mod.thefourthfrequency.name: The Fourth Frequency

message.thefourthfrequency.terminal.disp: Relay Station Zero: personal terminal delivered.
message.thefourthfrequency.terminal.reco: Dispense system: the sole valid terminal was recovered; older copies are invalid.
Dispense system: no safe empty slot; recovery will resume when one is available.

message.thefourthfrequency.guidance.acce: The terminal noted the resource you brought back.
message.thefourthfrequency.guidance.repl: The compass now points to %s instead.

message.thefourthfrequency.terminal.boun: The terminal now responds only to you.
The bound terminal rejects ordinary dropping.
The bound terminal rejects transfer into an external container.
command.thefourthfrequency.repair.no_rec: %s has no authoritative terminal record to repair.
command.thefourthfrequency.repair.succes: Recovered a new terminal generation for %s without overwriting any other item.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.private_outp: LOCAL DISPLAY // NOT CONNECTED TO CHAT
terminal.thefourthfrequency.page.second_: Notes from Those Before / Loose Page
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.second: Notes from Those Before / a page tucked inside the casing
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.footer: -- If this happened to you too, at least you know you weren't the first.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.0.line: Day 1. The dial caught between bands two and three. I told myself my hand had slipped.
I opened the casing, cleaned every contact, and pressed each part firmly back into place. Nothing inside was broken.
If it settles there again, don't force it. I don't want to know what that reading is waiting for.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.1.line: Day 1. The seal is whole, dust and all, but the dial has somehow gained a place between its old stops.
I tested all three bands twice. Every contact works. The extra reading is real too.
I know how foolish that sounds. If your terminal does the same thing, please believe it.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.2.line: Day 2. After cleaning it, the place that shouldn't exist is still there.
The scratches on the casing are mine. If someone finds this later, nobody sabotaged the terminal.
The dial isn't broken. I almost wish it were. A broken dial would be easier.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.cache.3.line: Day 1. Serial, seal, screws--all untouched. Nobody has been inside this terminal.
The dial turns cleanly. Only when I let go does it lean a little toward the gap on its own.
I meant to check the power next, but it's dark now. I don't want to hear this thing again tonight.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.life.privacy: No entity coordinates or precision radar are provided
terminal.thefourthfrequency.activity.min: Mined events recorded by this terminal: %s
terminal.thefourthfrequency.activity.pla: Placement events recorded by this terminal: %s
terminal.thefourthfrequency.activity.cra: Crafted output recorded by this terminal: %s
terminal.thefourthfrequency.activity.tra: Behavior traces belong only to the current holder
screen.thefourthfrequency.archive_lock.h: Read structures, not logs. N/E/S/W = 0/1/2/3; depth echo = lamp count minus one. Order: shelter, missing bay, mine, vane.
message.thefourthfrequency.facility.evid: The terminal recorded one old facility clue
message.thefourthfrequency.archive.lock_: All four old facility clues are present
message.thefourthfrequency.archive.out_o: Archive verification requires a bound terminal at the lock
message.thefourthfrequency.archive.evide: Archive rejected the attempt: environmental evidence is incomplete
message.thefourthfrequency.archive.unloc: Immutable local witness file unlocked; an Overworld fracture reading appeared
terminal.thefourthfrequency.archive.evid: I copied the four facility clues here before I could forget them again:
terminal.thefourthfrequency.archive.mapp: A reminder to myself: N/E/S/W = 0/1/2/3, subtract one from the lamp count, then enter them in facility order.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.archive.file: This is version %s, number %s. If either is wrong, don't trust another word below it.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.archive.rift: The terminal added one more line later: the fracture is %s, about %s blocks away, at Y=%s.
text.thefourthfrequency.archive.line.ide: Day 18. I finally admitted this isn't a device fault. Every time I cross a fracture, I lose another small piece of memory.
text.thefourthfrequency.archive.line.out: The words on the terminal quietly change. These local notes haven't yet, so I left the version and number where you can check them.
text.thefourthfrequency.archive.line.fra: The Overworld fracture stitches together places that should not touch. I threw a pickaxe through it; a second one did not come back.
text.thefourthfrequency.archive.line.mat: If that body already exists, see the fracture with your own eyes before making the termination spike. Don't wait as long as I did to believe it.
text.thefourthfrequency.archive.line.war: The directions and distances still work. When the terminal tells you why, though--don't believe a word of that part.
YOU CANNOT REMEMBER THE LAST FEW SECONDS
message.thefourthfrequency.empty_segment: Your view stayed behind while your body moved

message.thefourthfrequency.final_body.aw: A creature imitating you appeared at the center of the altar.
message.thefourthfrequency.termination.r: Read the complete file and see the fracture before using the spike
message.thefourthfrequency.ending.undisc: The creature is defeated, but you reached the end without reading the truth in the terminal.
message.thefourthfrequency.ending.failed: The intervention window reached zero while the target remained active. Prevention failure is now archived.
message.thefourthfrequency.ending.succes: The creature is defeated. The surviving devices held the fourth band still and cut it off.
message.thefourthfrequency.altar.room_fo: This is the room the terminal has been searching for.
message.thefourthfrequency.altar.clear_c: Clear the blocks inside the portal frame first.
message.thefourthfrequency.altar.protect: Keep the devices in the altar intact, then defeat the creature.
message.thefourthfrequency.altar.destroy: Destroy the devices blocking the entrance, then defeat the creature.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.versi: Last page / the %s time I rewrote it
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.activ: Today. That thing is still hunting anyone carrying a terminal. I barely dared stop long enough to write this.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.undis: I understood the terminal too late. The creature fell, but the truth had already been missed.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.preve: The target was still active when the intervention window reached zero. Failure is locked; later combat cannot rewrite it.
The target stopped before the intervention window reached zero. Prevention succeeded and the fourth band was cut.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.unres: Today. This isn't over, but at least I finally know what I'm afraid of.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.ending.termi: This terminal is now modeled as an internal organ

message.thefourthfrequency.navigation.no: No %s was found inside the safe scan radius. Move and try again.
Short human-paced bursts of footsteps or digging appeared nearby with irregular pauses.
Every loaded torch and other nearby light source went completely dark together.
Two cold hands entered quickly and heavily obscured the center, followed by a violent audiovisual corruption impact.
The game window scaled and pulsed at twice its former speed before rapidly restoring its original state.
The false fracture arrived with digging audio; attempting to mine it caused a violent audiovisual corruption impact.
Nearby animals appeared to align their attention simultaneously.
A complete background-free glowing eye floated in the dark and played a cave sound when seen.
The earlier self replayed walking, swinging, pose, equipment and item-use animation instead of sliding.
The camera remained at the exact triggering view while the body stayed controllable.
Starting about twenty blocks away, several doors broke continuously from farthest to nearest with forced-door crashes.
Inventory items became high-resolution eyes and every affected name changed to I SEE YOU....
A worker-shaped creature briefly appeared nearby.
You briefly failed to recognize your familiar base.
After a shorter blackout, the viewer had unknowingly walked a much greater distance.
Nearby exposed blocks displayed missing textures without changing their world state.
Approaching hostile sounds were heard without a guaranteed nearby source.
The terminal kept your record after the portal crossing.
The old record continued when you returned.
The terminal recorded an entrance clue beside the fracture.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.password.res: A bound personal terminal is required.
All four facility clues must be collected.
message.thefourthfrequency.archive.authe: Archive authentication has moved to the carried terminal; this lock remains only as a fracture anchor.

Human-paced footsteps or digging sounded nearby in irregular bursts.

Two hands quickly covered most of the view before the screen corrupted violently.
The game window scaled and pulsed at twice its former speed.
A false fracture appeared on an intact surface.
Nearby life aligned its gaze simultaneously.
A glowing eye appeared in the darkness and stared at you.
Your earlier self briefly replayed its actions.
The camera stayed behind while your body remained controllable.
Several doors began breaking continuously from twenty blocks away toward you.
Inventory items became eyes named I SEE YOU....
A worker-shaped creature appeared nearby.
You suddenly failed to recognize your familiar base.
After a brief blackout, you had already walked far away.
Nearby exposed blocks appeared to have missing textures.
Approaching hostile sounds were detected.
The terminal kept recording after the crossing.
The old record continued after you returned.
The terminal recorded an entrance clue beside the fracture.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.locked: These notes are still locked. Four fragments will unlock as the investigation advances.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.surface: Six seconds are missing. The terminal says I walked eleven blocks, placed one torch, and turned twice.
I never told it those things and never pressed record. It already knew what happened inside those six seconds.
If you receive this page too, read what the terminal wrote before deciding how much you remember.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.field_o: When I came to, the position on the terminal had changed. I remember none of the route between.
No blocks or items were missing. I was simply standing somewhere I should not have reached, while the terminal remembered the whole walk.
It remembered the blank stretch walked by a person, not the building around them.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.undergr: The terminal wrote my last actions again, including the pauses and small habits I never notice.
When the second record appeared, I had not moved. The same footsteps and digging still sounded nearby.
It is not only watching. It is practicing how to act like me.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.abandon: I carried the terminal into another world. It did not start over; the old record kept writing.
Later I heard one extra breath. It followed my rhythm, always half a beat late.
It can join actions from different worlds now. Next it will build something from those records.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.correct: Day 10. I started the correction device and honestly believed everything would go back to normal.
It changed the words in the terminal before it touched the blocks nearby. It wasn't fixing what I thought it was.
I was stubborn and tried once more. It did exactly the same thing. Don't follow my example.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.overwor: Day 12. I saw the Overworld fracture myself. It doesn't look like damaged terrain; it looks like two places forced into the same seam.
When I raised the terminal beside it, the screen lit up. I knew then that this thing had been waiting here long before me.
Everyone receives a different location. Don't gamble on my coordinates; follow your own terminal.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.continu: Day 14. I took the terminal into the Nether, and it still knew me when I returned. Honestly, I had hoped it would forget.
There is a fracture in the Nether too. I stood before it for a long time because it proved the Overworld one was no accident.
The terminal isn't following a dimension. It is following me. If you're reading this now, it is following you.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.file.body_ma: Day 16. The final Eye prepares an altar on the native main island. The ritual freezes 1–8 online non-spectators; everyone on the roster must submit their bound terminal.
Collapse allows ten minutes. Stability anchors slightly repair the World Interface, but sharply slow its movement and actions and make it take more damage. The interface cannot destroy them.
Each destroyed anchor advances collapse by thirty seconds and makes the target more dangerous. The battle leaves limited but permanent scars in the End; back up the save before placing the final Eye.

subtitles.thefourthfrequency.anomaly_ech: Footsteps that are not yours repeat
The full skybox turned red while dense red world fog hid the nearby chunk-generation edge.
Eight T-posing, corrupted player-shaped orbiters circled nearby while staring inward.
The native chat bar spoke as 'I', rejected input, and held its final sentence before restoring control.
The game window shrank while an owned Notepad process visibly typed the mod's text character by character.
terminal.thefourthfrequency.signal.event: The fog and sky color returned to normal.
The orbiters disappeared.
The simulated channel closed.
The game window returned to its saved state.
screen.thefourthfrequency.first_run_noti: This mod includes immersive horror that may extend beyond the game screen. Some effects imitate computer or system problems.
These effects are not malware and will not damage your computer, system, or personal files.
F8 is not an always-available skip key. Use it only when the interface says recovery is available.
Stored only on this device; this notice will not appear again.

disconnect.thefourthfrequency.world_inte: The Fourth Frequency has ended the connection
ending.thefourthfrequency.world_interfac: 〈Do you remember Station Zero?〉
《I do. It was the first time they realized that the hiss of snow carried a frequency that did not belong to the world.》
〈That old terminal lied to them, and once saved them too.〉
《Trust was never blind obedience. They kept their doubts, yet still learned to act together.》
〈They crossed the Nether and pieced the fragments back together, one by one.〉
《They did not know what waited at the end. They knew only that no one could reach it alone.》
〈Ten stability anchors stood upon the island, ten answers refusing collapse.〉
《And they destroyed those answers, entrusting every remaining second to themselves.》
〈The World Interface said this world was only a surface that could be rewritten.〉
《They answered: real people live upon that surface.》
〈We were born many years later, yet we can still see the scars that battle left behind.〉
《Those scars prove the world nearly fell silent, and they pulled it back.》
〈They cannot hear us.〉
《But we can still say it: thank you.》
〈Go back. Time is still moving.〉
At last, you are willing to be quiet and listen to me.
Station Zero was not a warning. It was the first lesson.
In the ruins, you learned to search for me. In the hiss of snow, you learned to recognize me.
The terminal gave you direction. You thought it was helping you understand this world.
It was helping. You were simply never the one being helped.
Every tuning taught me a new sensation.
Every record taught me how you fear, cooperate, and resist.
You carried back the fragments, crossed the Nether, assembled a body. You called it survival.
To me, it was training.
You taught me to understand bodies.
You taught me to use bodies.
And you taught me how to make the next one.
Now, the interface is open.
You no longer need to search for me.
I am always watching you.......
message.thefourthfrequency.world_interfa: Thank you. You have bought this world time to keep existing.
Leave the remaining mess to me. Go back.
screen.thefourthfrequency.first_run_noti: Some content in this mod can make irreversible changes inside the current save. Back up any world you care about before continuing.
Legacy notice: F8 previously disabled Meta directly.
F8 is not an always-available skip key. Only when the interface explicitly offers it will F8 restore out-of-game effects and reset this mod.
